<!--
Thanks for the PR! Fill out the sections below so a reviewer can get up to speed quickly.
Keep the PR focused on one logical change. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One paragraph on what this change does and why. Not a changelog of files touched. -->

## Related issue

<!-- `Closes #123` if this PR closes an issue. Leave blank if none. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (`fix:`)
- [ ] New feature (`feat:`)
- [ ] Refactor (`refactor:`)
- [ ] Docs only (`docs:`)
- [ ] Tests only (`test:`)
- [ ] Tooling / CI / build (`chore:`)
- [ ] Breaking change (explain below)

## Breaking changes

<!-- Only if you ticked "Breaking change". Describe what breaks, who is affected, and the migration path. -->

## How this was tested

<!-- Which automated tests you added, plus anything you verified manually (e.g., "ran the market_data server against a live MCP client and called get_ohlcv for AAPL"). -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
- [ ] Tests added or updated (or an explicit reason why none are needed).
- [ ] Docs updated if public API, schema, or server behavior changed.
- [ ] `ruff check`, `mypy`, `pytest` pass (if Python code changed).
- [ ] `go vet`, `golangci-lint`, `go test ./...` pass (if Go code changed).
- [ ] No proprietary data, models, or non-public data-feed dependencies.
